Candidate moves: left/right only translate without improving fit yet; soft/hard drop commits too early; rotate_left gives the other vertical orientation, less aligned with the visible notch. Best action now is rotate_right.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a vertical I in column 5 near the top. Surface below is uneven, but there is a useful 4-wide horizontal placement available around row 3 if rotated, which can help cover open space without immediately sealing critical wells. Goal is to place the I efficiently and set up for upcoming L/O/Z pieces; first sub-goal is getting the I into horizontal orientation before shifting/dropping later. Candidate actions: left/right only translate and miss the immediate need to reorient; soft/hard drop vertical I into column 5 creates a tall stack and less flexible placement; no_op wastes a turn. Rotating right changes the I to horizontal, opening better future placement options.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I currently horizontal near the top, spanning row 5 with open space beneath its columns except some clutter lower down. The stack is uneven with a central-left cavity, and no immediate line clear is available from an instant hard drop in the current orientation. Next pieces are L,O,Z,Z, so preserving a flatter surface and avoiding creating overhangs is preferable. Candidate actions: hard_drop would place the horizontal I awkwardly across uneven columns; soft_drop is committal without improving placement; left/right could reposition, but the vertical orientation appears more useful for filling a narrow column and threading into the central area. Rotating right converts the I to vertical in a valid position and is the best immediate setup move toward a cleaner drop on subsequent turns.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is L near the top above a flat I row, with open space on both sides. The stack is still low and there is no immediate danger, so the goal is to place the L efficiently to keep the surface smooth and avoid creating overhangs. A hard/soft drop now would commit to the current awkward column, while rotating in place changes shape but does not yet exploit the wide open lateral space. Shifting left is the best setup move because it moves the L toward the left-side gap above the long flat section, preserving options for a later drop or rotation into a cleaner fit.
